title: sorbus harrowiana
intro: Sorbus harrowiana, also known as Harrow whitebeam, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The health benefits of Sorbus harrowiana include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antiseptic, making it useful for wound care and skin issues. Herbal preparations of Sorbus harrowiana are typically made from the bark and leaves, and can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures.
